title: pinus finlaysoniana
intro: Pinus finlaysoniana, also known as Himalayan white pine, is a species of evergreen tree native to the eastern Himalayas and southern China. It is valued for its medicinal properties, with its bark, needles, and seeds used to treat various ailments, including bronchitis, asthma, and rheumatism due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Pinus finlaysoniana include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and acting as an antimicrobial agent, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be found in the form of essential oils and resin, which are used topically for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
